commit.message:
fix: remove certificate pinning from artifacts service

The artifacts service was using certificate pinning which caused
connection errors. Since artifacts are for public content sharing
and don't require the same security level as authentication endpoints,
switched to using a standard HTTP client.

This resolves certificate validation errors when accessing the
artifacts API endpoints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(skills): v3.7.56 — Skills 2.0 프로젝트별 자동 설치 시스템

## 변경 내용

### hive_api.py — /api/superpowers/* Skills 2.0 전환
- status: .claude/skills/vibe-{name}/SKILL.md 감지 (구 commands도 하위 호환)
- install: claude_skills/ → .claude/skills/ (copytree) + 구 .claude/commands/vibe-*.md 자동 마이그레이션
- uninstall: .claude/skills/vibe-*/ 디렉토리 제거 + 구 commands 잔재도 함께 정리

### hive_hook.py — 자동 설치 Skills 2.0 전환
- frozen: sys._MEIPASS/claude_skills/ → .claude/skills/<name>/ (누락 스킬만 복사)
- dev: 소스=대상 감지 → 복사 생략 (개발 환경 안전)
- UserPromptSubmit마다 실행 → 스킬 삭제 시 자동 복구

### build-release.yml — claude_skills 번들 추가
- xcopy .claude\skills .ai_monitor\claude_skills 추가
- --add-data "claude_skills;claude_skills" EXE에 포함 (2개 빌드 모두)

commit.message:
fix(auth): validate token against backend in layout beforeLoad (#24)

Replace the localStorage existence check with an actual API call to
GET /users/me in the protected layout's beforeLoad hook. Any failure
(expired token, revoked token, network error, empty localStorage) now
clears the stored token and redirects to /login, preventing stale
tokens from leaving users on a blank page.

commit.message:
fix: await auth initialization to restore login state

Previously authProvider.init() was called without await, causing the app
to start before the login session was restored from secure storage. This
resulted in users appearing logged out even though their credentials were
saved.

Now properly awaits the initialization to ensure login state is restored
before the app UI is displayed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: logos-blog
repository.description: Decentralized P2P blogging and RSS reader for Logos (Basecamp)

commit.message:
phase6: search, tag filter, profile view, markdown, rate limiting, OPML UI

C++ (headless blog_module):
- FeedStore: getPostsByTag() filters aggregated feed by tag
- FeedStore: per-author rate limiting (100 msgs/60s), 512 KB body cap, 1000 posts/author cap
- BlogPlugin: searchPosts(query) — scans own posts + feed for title/body/tag matches
- BlogPlugin: getPostsByTag(tag) — delegates to FeedStore
- BlogPlugin: getOpmlContent() — generates OPML 2.0 XML from subscriptions
- BlogPlugin: importOpml(xml) — parses OPML, subscribes to each <outline> with pubkey
- RssServer: markdownToHtml() improved — fenced code blocks (with lang class), blockquotes,
  ATX headings h1-h4, horizontal rules, unordered list items, images, links, bold+italic+code

C++ (blog_ui):
- BlogBackend: searchPosts(), getPostsByTag(), getOpmlContent(), importOpml()
- BlogBackend: exportOpmlToFile(fileUrl), importOpmlFromFile(fileUrl) for file I/O

QML:
- FeedView: search bar with live filtering, tag chip filter, result count,
  openAuthorProfile signal wired to PostCard.authorClicked
- ProfileView: new view — author avatar, name, bio, pubkey+copy, post count, post list
- Main: openAuthorProfile() pushes ProfileView, feeds FeedView.openAuthorProfile
- SettingsView: copy-URL buttons for each RSS feed URL; OPML export/import with
  FileDialog (QtQuick.Dialogs); confirmation dialog before unsubscribe
- blog_ui.qrc: ProfileView.qml added

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
phase5: RSS bridge — body parsing, POST /opml import, GET / index, improved OPML export

- onReadyRead now splits on \r\n\r\n to extract POST body correctly
- handleRequest accepts body param; routes POST /opml to importOpml()
- importOpml(): parse OPML 2.0 XML with QXmlStreamReader, subscribe to each <outline>
- serveIndex(): HTML index page listing all available feed URLs
- serveHealth() now returns JSON {"status":"ok"}
- OPML export includes dateCreated, htmlUrl, and uses configured bind address in URLs
- Feed URL base uses m_bind instead of hardcoded "localhost" across all serve* methods

commit.message:
feat: unify ResourcePool + security audit fixes

Task 03: ResourcePool unification (semaphore architecture consolidation)
- CAS-based single ResourcePool + Ticket (RAII auto-release)
- Remove tokio::Semaphore + MemoryPool entirely
- Switch decode_content to native format decoding
- WASM-side grayscale conversion (ITU-R BT.601)

Security audit fixes:
- Upgrade wasmtime 29→36 (RUSTSEC-2026-0006 f64.copysign sandbox escape)
- decode.rs: content-type agnostic decoder abstraction (bit-depth aware peak estimation)
- verify/handler.rs: early drop of intermediates (reduce peak memory)
- verify/handler.rs + sign/handler.rs: download global timeout
- sign/handler.rs: global timeout for entire processing loop (freeze protection)
- lib.rs: return -3 on metadata_ptr out-of-bounds (fix silent skip)
- lib.rs: move ownership into execute_inner (eliminate clone)
- phash-v1: add channels validation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Initial MT5 Fleet Orchestrator

Next.js App Router with PostgreSQL (Prisma) deployed on Railway.
Manages a fleet of Windows VPS instances running MT5 trading APIs.

Features:
- VPS fleet management (add, remove, provision via setup_vps.py)
- Account management across all VPS instances
- Trading proxy (buy/sell/close through orchestrator)
- Opposite copy trading controls
- Dashboard with aggregated fleet stats
- Background polling (30s) with historical snapshots
- VPS provisioning with SSE progress streaming

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

  left:44px (160px); Name sticky at left:204px (220px); z-40 headers / z-20 body;
  opaque row bg prevents bleed-through; record_id filtered at code level
- LeadsPage: Supabase postgres_changes real-time channel for INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE
  with surgical queryClient.setQueryData updates and channel cleanup on unmount

commit.message:
Add compile-time error for Debug builds to prevent cryptic linker failures

AEC3.lib is built in Release mode only. Debug builds previously produced
645 LNK2038 CRT mismatch errors — now they get one clear #error message
directing users to switch to Release|x64.

chore: remove upstream add-to-project workflow (#22)

This workflow was inherited from the FastAPI full-stack template and
adds issues/PRs to the upstream template's GitHub Project board. It
has no use in prata-sys and caused every CI run to fail on the
"Add to project" check due to the missing PROJECTS_TOKEN secret.

fix(quick-9): move yDomain after chartData to fix temporal dead zone error

commit.message:
phase3: WakuSync + FeedStore + Crypto — full P2P pipeline

- crypto.h/cpp: Ed25519 keygen, sign, verify via OpenSSL EVP_PKEY_ED25519
- WakuSync: add onDeliveryMessage() — decodes base64, emits messageReceived
- FeedStore: signature verification on all ingestion paths (ingestPost,
  ingestDelete, ingestProfile); last-write-wins conflict resolution using
  updated_at with lexicographic signature tie-break per spec
- FeedStore: ingestDelete/ingestProfile now accept full signed envelope
- BlogPlugin: loadOrCreateIdentity generates real Ed25519 keypair; caches
  m_ownPubkey/m_ownPrivkey for signing
- BlogPlugin: buildSignedEnvelope() builds canonical Waku envelope and
  signs with own privkey; used for post publish and delete tombstone
- BlogPlugin: connectDeliveryModule() wires api->on("delivery_module",
  "messageReceived") → subscription gate → WakuSync::onDeliveryMessage
- BlogPlugin: re-subscribes to all saved author topics on startup
- BlogPlugin: generateKeypair() Q_INVOKABLE for identity reset
- BlogPlugin: getIdentity() strips privkey from returned JSON
- CMakeLists: find_package(OpenSSL REQUIRED), OpenSSL::Crypto linked to
  both blog_module_plugin and blog_ui targets; crypto.cpp added to both

fix: avoid bash associative arrays with hyphenated keys in _ensure_labels

Bash treats hyphens in associative array key names as arithmetic subtraction,
causing "unbound variable" errors with set -u. Use simple colon-delimited
pairs instead.

commit.message:
fix(event): snapshot event before channel send to eliminate data race (#1422)

## What

`EmitEventWithTimeout` and `tryEmitReadyEvent` dereference `*e` for
logging **after** the event pointer has been sent on the channel. Once
`ch <- e` returns, the receiving goroutine owns the pointer and may
write to it concurrently — `runner.copyEventInvocationFields` copies
invocation metadata into the same struct while the sender's
`log.TracefContext`
call is still reading it.

`go test -race` reports this reliably:

```
DATA RACE
Write at runner/runner.go:841  (copyEventInvocationFields — writes ParentInvocationID)
Read  at event/event.go:280    (tryEmitReadyEvent — reads *e for TracefContext)
```

## Fix

Snapshot `fmt.Sprintf("%+v", *e)` **before** each of the three
`ch <- e` send sites. The snapshot happens while the caller still
holds exclusive ownership of the struct; the log statement after the
send uses the pre-captured string and never touches `*e` again.

```go
// before
case ch <- e:
    log.TracefContext(ctx, "event sent: %+v", *e)  // race: receiver may mutate *e

// after
eventStr := fmt.Sprintf("%+v", *e)  // safe: still sole owner
case ch <- e:
    log.TracefContext(ctx, "event sent: %s", eventStr)  // no *e access post-send
```

No behaviour change — the logged content is identical; only the timing
of the read shifts from after-send to before-send.

## Reproduction

Any test that wires `runner.NewRunner` with a plugin and uses `go test
-race` triggers the race. The three affected sites are at lines 280,
327, and 349 of `event/event.go`.

---

First of all — thank you for building trpc-agent-go. The plugin and
event architecture is genuinely well-designed and a pleasure to build on
top of. This is a small fix to an otherwise rock-solid framework.

## Summary by Sourcery

错误修复：
- 通过在所有 `EmitEvent`/`EmitEventWithTimeout`
路径中，在通过通道发送之前先将事件格式化为字符串，防止在日志记录期间对 `Event` 结构体的并发访问。

<details>
<summary>Original summary in English</summary>

## Summary by Sourcery

Bug Fixes:
- Prevent concurrent access to Event structs during logging by
formatting the event into a string before channel send in all
EmitEvent/EmitEventWithTimeout paths.

</details>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: trpc-agent-go
repository.description: trpc-agent-go is a powerful Go framework for building intelligent agent systems using large language models (LLMs) and tools.
